---
title: "VESM"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
    toc: false
    number_sections: false
    fig_caption: yes
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{VESM}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
---
# Overview
```{r model_family_header, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
acousticTS:::.model_family_header(
  family = "vesm",
  pages = c(
    Overview = "index.html",
    Implementation = "vesm-implementation.html",
    Theory = "vesm-theory.html"
  )
)
```

```{r vesm_overview, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
acousticTS:::.model_family_overview(
  family = "vesm",
  summary = paste(
    "The viscous-elastic spherical model (`VESM`) is a layered spherical",
    "resonance family for a gas core surrounded by an elastic shell and an",
    "outer viscous biological layer."
  ),
  core_idea = paste(
    "Solve the spherical layered problem mode by mode, with acoustic waves in",
    "the exterior and gas core, viscous compressional/shear branches in the",
    "soft outer layer, and elastic longitudinal/transverse waves in the shell."
  ),
  best_for = c(
    "Gas-bearing mesopelagic-fish style layered spheres",
    "Problems where gas resonance, shell elasticity, and viscous damping all matter simultaneously",
    "Wideband spherical resonance studies beyond a simple bubble or shell-only idealization"
  ),
  supports = c(
    "`ESS` spherical objects with an explicit shell and inner gas core plus viscous-layer model arguments",
    "Four-region spherical bookkeeping with seawater as medium `1`",
    "Monostatic layered resonance calculations"
  ),
  assumptions = c(
    "Spherical symmetry",
    "Homogeneous material properties within each layer",
    "Linear acoustics and linear elasticity",
    "Layered spherical interfaces with no nonspherical posture or geometry effects"
  ),
  reading = c(
    "[Implementation](vesm-implementation.html): layered-object workflow and benchmark comparisons",
    "[Theory](vesm-theory.html): full layered spherical field structure, interface conditions, and mode-wise systems"
  )
)
```
